paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: For centuries, historians and archaeologists have puzzled over the many mysteries of Stonehenge, a prehistoric monument that took an estimated 1,500 years to erect. Located on Salisbury Plain in southern England, it is comprised of roughly 100 massive upright stones placed in a circular layout.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Archaeologists believe England's most iconic prehistoric ruin was built in several stages, with the earliest constructed 5,000 or more years ago. First, Neolithic* Britons used primitive tools, which may have been fashioned out of deer antlers, to dig a massive circular ditch and bank, or henge. Deep pits dating back to that era and located within the circle may have once held a ring of timber posts, according to some scholars.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Several hundred years later, it is thought, Stonehenge's builders hoisted an estimated 80 bluestones, 43 of which remain today, into standing positions and placed them in either a horseshoe or circular formation. These stones have been traced all the way to the Preseli Hills in Wales, some 300 kilometres from Stonehenge. How, then, did prehistoric builders without sophisticated tools or engineering haul these boulders, which weigh up to four tons, over such a great distance?

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: According to one long-standing theory among archaeologists, Stonehenge's builders fashioned sledges and rollers out of tree trunks to lug the bluestones from the Preseli Hills. They then transferred the boulders onto rafts and floated them first along the Welsh coast and then up the River Avon toward Salisbury Plain; alternatively, they may have towed each stone with a fleet of vessels. More recent archaeological hypotheses have them transporting the bluestones with supersized wicker baskets on a combination of ball bearings and long grooved planks, hauled by oxen.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: As early as the 1970s, geologists have been adding their voices to the debate over how Stonehenge came into being. Challenging the classic image of industrious builders pushing, carting, rolling or hauling giant stones from faraway Wales, some scientists have suggested that it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain. Most archaeologists have remained sceptical about this theory, however, wondering how the forces of nature could possibly have delivered the exact number of stones needed to complete the circle.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The third phase of construction took place around 2000 BCE. At this point, sandstone slabs – known as 'sarsens' – were arranged into an outer crescent or ring; some were assembled into the iconic three-piece structures called trilithons that stand tall in the centre of Stonehenge. Some 50 of these stones are now visible on the site, which may once have contained many more. Radiocarbon dating has revealed that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE, with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: But who were the builders of Stonehenge? In the 17th century, archaeologist John Aubrey made the claim that Stonehenge was the work of druids, who had important religious, judicial and political roles in Celtic** society. This theory was widely popularized by the antiquarian William Stukeley, who had unearthed primitive graves at the site. Even today, people who identify as modern druids continue to gather at Stonehenge for the summer solstice. However, in the mid-20th century, radiocarbon dating demonstrated that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts inhabited the region.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Many modern historians and archaeologists now agree that several distinct tribes of people contributed to Stonehenge, each undertaking a different phase of its construction. Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site seem to support this hypothesis. The first stage was achieved by Neolithic agrarians who were likely to have been indigenous to the British Isles. Later, it is believed, groups with advanced tools and a more communal way of life left their mark on the site. Some believe that they were immigrants from the European continent, while others maintain that they were probably native Britons, descended from the original builders.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: If the facts surrounding the architects and construction of Stonehenge remain shadowy at best, the purpose of the striking monument is even more of a mystery. While there is consensus among the majority of modern scholars that Stonehenge once served the function of burial ground, they have yet to determine what other purposes it had.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: In the 1960s, the astronomer Gerald Hawkins suggested that the cluster of megalithic stones operated as a form of calendar, with different points corresponding to astrological phenomena such as solstices, equinoxes and eclipses occurring at different times of the year. While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades, critics maintain that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: More recently, signs of illness and injury in the human remains unearthed at Stonehenge led a group of British archaeologists to speculate that it was considered a place of healing, perhaps because bluestones were thought to have curative powers.

central_sentences.sentence: First, Neolithic* Britons used primitive tools, which may have been fashioned out of deer antlers, to dig a massive circular ditch and bank, or henge. Deep

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "timber posts" 或 "posts"。题干中的 "arranged" 对应文章中的 "held"，"deep pits" 对应文章中的 "Deep pits"，"inside the circle" 对应文章中的 "within the circle"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案在这句话中。文章明确提到这些深坑可能曾经放置了一圈木桩，因此答案是 "timber posts" 或 "posts"。

central_sentences.sentence: Deep pits dating back to that era and located within the circle may have once held a ring of timber posts, according to some scholars.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "builders used" 对应文章中的 "Stonehenge's builders fashioned"，"sledges and rollers" 在题干和文章中都是相同的表达。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案句中的 "tree trunks" 是用来制作 "sledges and rollers" 的材料。因此，正确答案是 "tree trunks"。

central_sentences.sentence: According to one long-standing theory among archaeologists, Stonehenge's builders fashioned sledges and rollers out of tree trunks to lug the bluestones from the Preseli Hills.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“pulled them on giant baskets”与原文中的“hauled by oxen”相对应。题干中的“pulled”在原文中被替换为“hauled”，“baskets”被替换为“wicker baskets”。因此，正确答案是“oxen”，因为它是拉动篮子的主体。

central_sentences.sentence: More recent archaeological hypotheses have them transporting the bluestones with supersized wicker baskets on a combination of ball bearings and long grooved planks, hauled by oxen.
central_sentences.translation: 最近的考古假说认为，他们使用超大型柳条篮子在球轴承和长沟槽木板的组合上运输蓝石，由牛拉动。

questions.analysis: 答案是 "glaciers"。题干中的 "they were brought from Wales by" 对应文章中的 "carried the bluestones from faraway Wales" 和 "by"。文章明确提到 "some scientists have suggested that it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain"，这与题干中的描述完全一致。因此，正确答案是 "glaciers"。

central_sentences.sentence: Challenging the classic image of industrious builders pushing, carting, rolling or hauling giant stones from faraway Wales, some scientists have suggested that it was glaciers, not humans, that carried the bluestones to Salisbury Plain.
central_sentences.translation: 一些科学家对勤劳的建造者推、拉、滚动或搬运巨石从遥远的威尔士运来的传统观点提出质疑，他们认为是冰川而不是人类将蓝石运到了索尔兹伯里平原。

questions.analysis: 题干中的“17th century”和“Celtic”在文章中分别对应“In the 17th century”和“Celtic society”。根据文章内容，德鲁伊（druids）在凯尔特社会中有重要角色，因此题干中的空格应填入“druids”。

central_sentences.sentence: In the 17th century, archaeologist John Aubrey made the claim that Stonehenge was the work of druids, who had important religious, judicial and political roles in Celtic** society
central_sentences.translation: 在17世纪，考古学家John Aubrey声称巨石阵是德鲁伊的作品，他们在凯尔特社会中扮演着重要的宗教、司法和政治角色。

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "many experts agree" 对应原文中的 "consensus among the majority of modern scholars"，而 "used as a ... site" 对应 "served the function of ... ground"。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到答案句。答案句明确指出，巨石阵曾经起到了 "burial ground"（墓地）的作用，因此正确答案是 "burial"。

central_sentences.sentence: While there is consensus among the majority of modern scholars that Stonehenge once served the function of burial ground, they have yet to determine what other purposes it had.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然大多数现代学者一致认为巨石阵曾经用作墓地，但他们还没有确定它的其他用途。

questions.analysis: 答案 "calendar" 是正确的，因为题干中的 "in the 1960s" 和 "suggested that it worked as a kind of" 在原文中分别对应 "In the 1960s" 和 "suggested that ... operated as a form of"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以定位到答案句，并发现 "calendar" 是描述这些巨石群功能的词汇。

central_sentences.sentence: In the 1960s, the astronomer Gerald Hawkins suggested that the cluster of megalithic stones operated as a form of calendar, with different points corresponding to astrological phenomena such as solstices, equinoxes and eclipses occurring at different times of the year.
central_sentences.translation: 在20世纪60年代，天文学家杰拉尔德·霍金斯提出，这群巨石运作起来就像一种日历，其中不同的点对应着一年中不同时间发生的天文现象，如冬至、夏至、春分、秋分和日食等。

questions.title: During the third phase of construction, sandstone slabs were placed in both the outer areas and the middle of the Stonehenge site.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "During the third phase of construction, sandstone slabs were placed in both the outer areas and the middle of the Stonehenge site." 在原文中得到了直接的支持。原文提到 "The third phase of construction" 对应题干中的 "third phase of construction"，"sandstone slabs – known as 'sarsens'" 对应题干中的 "sandstone slabs"，"outer crescent or ring" 对应题干中的 "outer areas"，"centre" 对应题干中的 "middle"。因此，题干中的信息在原文中找到了明确的对应，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: At this point, sandstone slabs – known as 'sarsens' – were arranged into an outer crescent or ring; some were assembled into the iconic three-pieced structures called trilithons that stand tall in the centre of Stonehenge.
central_sentences.translation: 在这个阶段，被称为"巨石"的砂岩板被排列成外部的新月形或环形；一些被组装成标志性的三块一组的结构，称为三石柱，矗立在巨石阵的中心。

questions.title: There is scientific proof that the bluestones stood in the same spot until approximately 1600 BCE.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息与文章中的事实相矛盾。题干声称蓝石一直在同一个地方，而文章明确指出蓝石被多次重新安置。因此，题目的正确答案是“FALSE”。

central_sentences.sentence: Radiocarbon dating has revealed that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE, with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times.
central_sentences.translation: 放射性碳定年法显示，巨石阵的建造工作一直持续到大约公元前1600年，特别是蓝石在这期间被多次重新安置。

target_sen.target_sen: Radiocarbon dating has revealed that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE, with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times.
Radiocarbon dating has revealed that work continued at Stonehenge until roughly 1600 BCE, with the bluestones in particular being repositioned multiple times.

questions.title: John Aubrey's claim about Stonehenge was supported by 20th-century findings.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“John Aubrey's claim”在原文中对应“John Aubrey made the claim”，题干中的“20th-century findings”在原文中对应“in the mid-20th century, radiocarbon dating demonstrated”，题干中的“supported”在原文中对应“demonstrated that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts inhabited the region”。原文明确指出20世纪的发现（放射性碳定年法）否定了John Aubrey的主张，因此答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: However, in the mid-20th century, radiocarbon dating demonstrated that Stonehenge stood more than 1,000 years before the Celts inhabited the region.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，在20世纪中期，放射性碳定年法证明巨石阵的建造时间比凯尔特人居住在该地区的时间早1000多年。

questions.title: Objects discovered at Stonehenge seem to indicate that it was constructed by a number of different groups of people.
questions.analysis: 答案是TRUE，因为题干中的“Objects discovered”对应原文中的“Bones, tools and other artefacts found”，而“different groups of people”对应“several distinct tribes of people”。原文明确提到这些发现的物品支持了由不同部落建造巨石阵的假设，这与题干信息一致。

central_sentences.sentence: Many modern historians and archaeologists now agree that several distinct tribes of people contributed to Stonehenge, each undertaking a different phase of its construction. Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site seem to support this hypothesis.
central_sentences.translation: 许多现代历史学家和考古学家现在一致认为，数个不同的部落参与了巨石阵的建造，每个部落负责不同的建造阶段。在遗址上发现的骨骼、工具和其他文物似乎支持了这一假说。

syn_ary.synonym: Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site

target_sen.target_sen: Bones, tools and other artefacts found on the site seem to support this hypothesis.
Many modern historians and archaeologists now agree that several distinct tribes of people contributed to Stonehenge, each undertaking a different phase of its construction.

questions.title: Criticism of Gerald Hawkins' theory about Stonehenge has come mainly from other astronomers.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词包括“criticism”、“Gerald Hawkins' theory”和“astronomers”。在原文中，"criticism"对应的是"critics"，"Gerald Hawkins' theory"对应的是"his theory"。然而，原文中并没有提到批评者是天文学家（astronomers）。因此，题干中的信息在原文中找不到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades, critics maintain that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管他的理论在几十年里受到了相当多的关注，但批评者认为，斯通亨奇的建造者可能缺乏预测这些事件所需的知识，或者英格兰的浓密云层可能会遮挡他们对天空的视线。

target_sen.target_sen: While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades, critics maintain that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies.

While his theory has received a considerable amount of attention over the decades, critics maintain that Stonehenge's builders probably lacked the knowledge necessary to predict such events or that England's dense cloud cover would have obscured their view of the skies.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: This has been the decade of AI, with one astonishing feat after another. A chess-playing AI that can defeat not only all human chess players, but also all previous human-programmed chess machines, after learning the game in just four hours? That's yesterday's news, what's next? True, these prodigious accomplishments are all in so-called narrow AI, where machines perform highly specialised tasks. But many experts believe this restriction is very temporary. By mid-century, we may have artificial general intelligence (AGI) – machines that can achieve human-level performance on the full range of tasks that we ourselves can tackle.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: If so, there's little reason to think it will stop there. Machines will be free of many of the physical constraints on human intelligence. Our brains run at slow biochemical processing speeds on the power of a light bulb, and their size is restricted by the dimensions of the human birth canal. It is remarkable what they accomplish, given these handicaps. But they may be as far from the physical limits of thought as our eyes are from the incredibly powerful Webb Space Telescope.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Once machines are better than us at designing even smarter machines, progress towards these limits could accelerate. What would this mean for us? Could we ensure a safe and worthwhile coexistence with such machines? On the plus side, AI is already useful and profitable for many things, and super AI might be expected to be super useful and super profitable. But the more powerful AI becomes, the more important it will be to specify its goals with great care. Folklore is full of tales of people who ask for the wrong thing, with disastrous consequences – King Midas, for example, might have wished that everything he touched turned to gold, but didn't really intend this to apply to his breakfast.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: So we need to create powerful AI machines that are 'human-friendly' – that have goals reliably aligned with our own values. One thing that makes this task difficult is that we are far from reliably human-friendly ourselves. We do many terrible things to each other and to many other creatures with whom we share the planet. If superintelligent machines don't do a lot better than us, we'll be in deep trouble. We'll have powerful new intelligence amplifying the dark sides of our own fallible natures.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: For safety's sake, then, we want the machines to be ethically as well as cognitively superhuman. We want them to aim for the moral high ground, not for the troughs in which many of us spend some of our time. Luckily, they'll be smart enough for the job. If there are routes to the moral high ground, they'll be better than us at finding them and steering us in the right direction.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: However, there are two big problems with this utopian vision. One is how we get the machines started on the journey; the other is what it would mean to reach this destination. The 'getting started' problem is that we need to tell the machines what they're looking for with sufficient clarity that we can be confident they will find it – whatever 'it' actually turns out to be. This won't be easy, given that we are tribal creatures and conflicted about the ideals ourselves. We often ignore the suffering of strangers and even contribute to it, at least indirectly. How then do we point machines in the direction of something better?

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: As for the 'destination' problem, we might, by putting ourselves in the hands of these moral guides and gatekeepers, be sacrificing our own autonomy – an important part of what makes us human. Machines who are better than us at sticking to the moral high ground may be expected to discourage some of the lapses we presently take for granted. We might lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities, for example.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Loss of freedom to behave badly isn't always a bad thing, of course: denying ourselves the freedom to put children to work in factories or to smoke in restaurants are signs of progress. But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options? They might be so good at doing it that we won't notice them; but few of us are likely to welcome such a future.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: These issues might seem far-fetched, but they are to some extent already here. AI already has some input into how resources are used in our National Health Service (NHS) here in the UK, for example. If it was given a greater role, it might do so much more efficiently than humans can manage and act in the interests of taxpayers and those who use the health system. However, we'd be depriving some humans (e.g., senior doctors) of the control they presently enjoy. Since we'd want to ensure that people are treated equally and that policies are fair, the goals of AI would need to be specified correctly.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: We have a new powerful technology to deal with – itself, literally, a new way of thinking. For our own safety, we need to point these new thinkers in the right direction and get them to act well for us. It is not yet clear whether this is possible, but if it is, it will require a cooperative spirit and a willingness to set aside self-interest.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Both general intelligence and moral reasoning are often thought to be uniquely human capacities. But safety seems to require that we think of them as a package: if we are to give general intelligence to machines, we'll need to give them moral authority, too. And where exactly would that leave human beings? All the more reason to think about the destination now and to be careful about what we wish for.

groups.desc:
Questions 14–19

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: What point does the writer make about AI in the first paragraph?

options.text: It is difficult to predict how quickly AI will progress.
Much can be learned about the use of AI in chess machines.
The future is unlikely to see limitations on the capabilities of AI.
Experts disagree on which specialised tasks AI will be able to perform.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到许多专家认为当前的限制是暂时的，并且未来可能会有人工通用智能（AGI），这表明未来AI的能力不太可能受到限制。因此，选项C是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: But many experts believe this restriction is very temporary. By mid-century, we may have artificial general intelligence (AGI) – machines that can achieve human-level performance on the full range of tasks that we ourselves can tackle.
central_sentences.translation: 但许多专家认为这种限制是非常暂时的。到本世纪中叶，我们可能会有人工通用智能（AGI）——能够在我们自己能处理的全部任务范围内达到人类水平表现的机器。

questions.title: What is the writer doing in the second paragraph?

options.text: explaining why machines will be able to outperform humans
describing the characteristics that humans and machines share
giving information about the development of machine intelligence
indicating which aspects of humans are the most advanced

questions.analysis: 在第二段中，作者解释了为什么机器能够超越人类。作者提到机器没有人类大脑的物理限制，如生化处理速度慢和大脑尺寸受限于产道尺寸等。因此，机器能够在这些方面超越人类。这与选项A“解释为什么机器能够超越人类”直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Machines will be free of many of the physical constraints on human intelligence.
central_sentences.translation: 机器将不受制于人类智力的许多物理限制。

questions.title: Why does the writer mention the story of King Midas?

options.text: to compare different visions of progress
to illustrate that poorly defined objectives can go wrong
to emphasise the need for cooperation
to point out the financial advantages of a course of action

questions.analysis: 作者提到Midas国王的故事是为了说明如果目标定义不明确，会导致严重的问题。这与正确答案B的意思完全一致，即“为了说明目标定义不明确会出问题”。

central_sentences.sentence: Folklore is full of tales of people who ask for the wrong thing, with disastrous consequences – King Midas, for example, might have wished that everything he touched turned to gold, but didn't really intend this to apply to his breakfast.
central_sentences.translation: 民间传说中充满了人们提出错误要求而导致灾难性后果的故事——例如，米达斯国王可能希望他触摸的一切都变成黄金，但他并不真的想让这个愿望应用到他的早餐上。

target_sen.target_sen: Folklore is full of tales of people who ask for the wrong thing, with disastrous consequences – King Midas, for example, might have wished that everything he touched turned to gold, but didn't really intend this to apply to his breakfast.

questions.title: What challenge does the writer refer to in the fourth paragraph?

options.text: encouraging humans to behave in a more principled way
deciding which values we want AI to share with us
creating a better world for all creatures on the planet
ensuring AI is more human-friendly than we are ourselves

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D，因为文章中明确提到的挑战是确保AI比我们更“human-friendly”。文章中提到“如果超级智能机器不能比我们做得好很多，我们将陷入深深的麻烦”，这与选项D中的“确保AI比我们更人性化”意思相同。

central_sentences.sentence: If superintelligent machines don't do a lot better than us, we'll be in deep trouble.
central_sentences.translation: 如果超级智能机器不能比我们做得更好，我们就会陷入深重的麻烦。

target_sen.target_sen: One thing that makes this task difficult is that we are far from reliably human-friendly ourselves.

questions.title: What does the writer suggest about the future of AI in the fifth paragraph?

options.text: The safety of machines will become a key issue.
It is hard to know what impact machines will have on the world.
Machines will be superior to humans in certain respects.
Many humans will oppose machines having a wider role.

questions.analysis: 文章中明确提到机器在道德和认知方面会超越人类，并且会比我们更擅长找到道德高地。这与选项C“Machines will be superior to humans in certain respects”意思相符，因此选项C是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: We want them to aim for the moral high ground, not for the troughs in which many of us spend some of our time. Luckily they'll be smart enough for the job. If there are routes to the moral high ground, they'll be better than us at finding them, and steering us in the right direction.
central_sentences.translation: 我们希望它们瞄准道德高地，而不是我们中许多人有时所处的低谷。幸运的是，它们将足够聪明来完成这项任务。如果有通往道德高地的路径，它们会比我们更擅长找到这些路径，并引导我们朝着正确的方向前进。

syn_ary.synonym: machines to be ethically as well as cognitively superhuman

target_sen.target_sen: For safety's sake, then, we want the machines to be ethically as well as cognitively superhuman.

questions.title: Which of the following best summarises the writer's argument in the sixth paragraph?

options.text: More intelligent machines will result in greater abuses of power.
Machine learning will share very few features with human learning.
There are a limited number of people with the knowledge to program machines.
Human shortcomings will make creating the machines we need more difficult.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是D。文章第六段主要讨论了人类的缺陷如何使得创建我们所需的机器变得更加困难。作者提到我们需要以足够的清晰度告诉机器它们在寻找什么，但这并不容易，因为我们人类本身就有很多缺陷，如部落性和对理想的矛盾态度，以及忽视陌生人的痛苦。这些都使得创建机器变得更加困难。因此，选项D最能总结作者在第六段中的论点。

central_sentences.sentence: we need to tell the machines what they're looking for with sufficient clarity that we can be confident they will find it – whatever 'it' actually turns out to be. This won't be easy, given that we are tribal creatures and conflicted about the ideals ourselves. We
central_sentences.translation: 我们需要以足够清晰的方式告诉机器它们在寻找什么，以便我们能够确信它们会找到目标——无论"它"最终是什么。考虑到我们是部落生物，对理想本身也存在矛盾，这并不容易。

groups.desc:
Questions 20–23

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Machines with the ability to make moral decisions may prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Machines with the ability to make moral decisions may prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities”在原文中有对应的表达。原文中提到“We might lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities, for example.”，这句话的意思是我们可能会失去偏袒自己社区利益的自由，与题干中的“prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities”意思相同。因此，答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: We might lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities, for example.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，我们可能会失去偏袒自己社区利益的自由。

syn_ary.synonym: machines who are better than us at sticking to the moral high ground

target_sen.target_sen: Machines who are better than us at sticking to the moral high ground may be expected to discourage some of the lapses we presently take for granted.

locating_words.locating_word: Machines with the ability to make moral decisions

syn_ary.synonym: lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities

target_sen.target_sen: We might lose our freedom to discriminate in favour of our own communities, for example.

locating_words.locating_word: prevent us from promoting the interests of our communities

questions.title: Silicon police would need to exist in large numbers in order
questions.analysis: 题干问的是“硅警察是否需要存在于大量”。文章提到了“ethical silicon police”这一概念，但并没有讨论数量问题。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中并没有提及与题干中数量相关的信息。

central_sentences.sentence: But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options? They might be so good at doing it that we won't notice them; but few of us are likely to welcome such a future.
central_sentences.translation: 但我们是否已经准备好接受道德硅警察限制我们的选择？他们可能会做得如此出色，以至于我们不会注意到他们；但很少有人会欢迎这样的未来。

target_sen.target_sen: But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options?

questions.title: Many people are comfortable with the prospect of their independence being restricted by machines.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“comfortable”在原文中对应的是“welcome”，“independence”对应的是“autonomy”，“restricted”对应的是“limiting”，“machines”在原文中直接出现。原文中明确提到“few of us are likely to welcome such a future”，意思是“我们中的很少人会欢迎这样的未来”，这与题干中“许多人对他们的独立性被机器限制的前景感到舒服”是相反的。因此，正确答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options? They might be so good at doing it that we won't notice them; but few of us are likely to welcome such a future.
central_sentences.translation: 但我们准备好接受道德硅制警察限制我们的选择了吗？他们可能会做得如此好以至于我们都不会注意到他们；但我们中很少有人可能会欢迎这样的未来。

target_sen.target_sen: They might be so good at doing it that we won't notice them; but few of us are likely to welcome such a future.
But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options?
But are we ready for ethical silicon police limiting our options?

questions.title: If we want to ensure that machines act in our best interests, we all need to work together.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 YES，因为题干中的 "ensure that machines act in our best interests" 对应原文中的 "get them to act well for us"，而 "we all need to work together" 对应原文中的 "cooperative spirit"。原文明确提到，为了确保机器为我们的利益行事，我们需要合作精神，这与题干的意思完全一致。因此，答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: For our own safety, we need to point these new thinkers in the right direction, and get them to act well for us. It is not yet clear whether this is possible, but if it is, it will require a cooperative spirit, and a willingness to set aside self-interest.
central_sentences.translation: 为了我们自身的安全，我们需要引导这些新型思考者朝正确的方向发展，并使它们为我们行善。目前还不清楚这是否可能，但如果可能的话，这将需要合作精神，以及愿意放下自身利益。

syn_ary.synonym: point these new thinkers in the right direction, and get them to act well for us

target_sen.target_sen: For our own safety, we need to point these new thinkers in the right direction, and get them to act well for us.

locating_words.locating_word: ensure that machines act in our best interests

target_sen.target_sen: It is not yet clear whether this is possible, but if it is, it will require a cooperative spirit, and a willingness to set aside self-interest.

groups.title: Using AI in the UK health system
groups.desc:
Questions 24–26

Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–F, below.
groups.collect: AI currently has a limited role in the way$64$ are allocated in the health service. The positive aspect of AI having a bigger role is that it would be more efficient and lead to patient benefits. However, such a change would result, for example, in certain$65$ not having their current level of $66$. It is therefore important that AI goals are appropriate so that discriminatory practices could be avoided.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“allocated”在原文中对应的同义词是“used”，“health service”在原文中对应的是“National Health Service (NHS)”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案是“available resources”。

central_sentences.sentence: AI already has some input into how resources are used in our National Health Service (NHS) here in the UK, for example.

target_sen.target_sen: senior doctors) of the control they presently enjoy.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "current level of" 对应原文中的 "presently enjoy"，而 "not having" 对应 "depriving"。原文提到高级医生将失去他们目前享有的控制权，这与题干中的 "certain not having their current level of" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案是 "professional authority"（专业权威），因为这最能反映高级医生失去的控制权。

central_sentences.sentence: However, we'd be depriving some humans (e.g. senior doctors) of the control they presently enjoy.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The word 'genius' is universally associated with the name of Leonardo da Vinci. A true Renaissance man, he embodied scientific spirit, artistic talent and humanist sensibilities. Five hundred years have passed since Leonardo died in his home at Château du Clos Lucé, outside Tours, France. Yet far from fading into insignificance, his thinking has carried down the centuries and still surprises today.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The Renaissance marked the transition from the 15th century to modernity and took place after the spread of the plague in the 14th century, which caused a global crisis resulting in some 200 million deaths across Europe and Asia. Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed. Then, as now, radical solutions were called for to revolutionise the way people lived and safeguard humanity against catastrophe.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Around 1486 – after a pestilence that killed half the population in Milan, Italy – Leonardo turned his thoughts to urban planning problems. Following a typical Renaissance trend, he began to work on an 'ideal city' project, which – due to its excessive costs – would remain unfulfilled. Yet given that unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today, it's only natural to wonder how Leonardo might have changed the shape of modern cities.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Although the Renaissance is renowned as an era of incredible progress in art and architecture, it is rarely noted that the 15th century also marked the birth of urbanism as a true academic discipline. The rigour and method behind the conscious conception of a city had been largely missing in Western thought until the moment when prominent Renaissance men pushed forward large-scale urban projects in Italy, such as the reconfiguration of the town of Pienza and the expansion of the city of Ferrara. These works surely inspired Leonardo's decision to rethink the design of medieval cities, with their winding and overcrowded streets and with houses piled against one another.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: It is not easy to identify a coordinated vision of Leonardo's ideal city because of his disordered way of working with notes and sketches. But from the largest collection of Leonardo's papers ever assembled, a series of innovative thoughts can be reconstructed regarding the foundation of a new city along the Ticino River, which runs from Switzerland into Italy and is 248 kilometres long. He designed the city for the easy transport of goods and clean urban spaces, and he wanted a comfortable and spacious city, with well-ordered streets and architecture. He recommended 'high, strong walls', with 'towers and battlements of all necessary and pleasant beauty'.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: His plans for a modern and 'rational' city were consistent with Renaissance ideals. But, in keeping with his personality, Leonardo included several innovations in his urban design. Leonardo wanted the city to be built on several levels, linked with vertical outdoor staircases. This design can be seen in some of today's high-rise buildings but was unconventional at the time. Indeed, this idea of taking full advantage of the interior spaces wasn't implemented until the 1920s and 1930s, with the birth of the Modernist movement.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: While in the upper layers of the city, people could walk undisturbed between elegant palaces and streets, the lower layer was the place for services, trade, transport and industry. But the true originality of Leonardo's vision was its fusion of architecture and engineering. Leonardo designed extensive hydraulic plants to create artificial canals throughout the city. The canals, regulated by clocks and basins, were supposed to make it easier for boats to navigate inland. Leonardo also thought that the width of the streets ought to match the average height of the adjacent houses: a rule still followed in many contemporary cities across Italy, to allow access to sun and reduce the risk of damage from earthquakes.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Although some of these features existed in Roman cities, before Leonardo's drawings there had never been a multi-level, compact modern city which was thoroughly technically conceived. Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied. For example, the subdivision of the city by function – with services and infrastructures located in the lower levels and wide and well-ventilated boulevards and walkways above for residents – is an idea that can be found in Georges-Eugène Haussmann's renovation of Paris under Emperor Napoleon III between 1853 and 1870.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Today, Leonardo's ideas are not simply valid, they actually suggest a way forward for urban planning. Many scholars think that the compact city, built upwards instead of outwards, integrated with nature (especially water systems), with efficient transport infrastructure, could help modern cities become more efficient and sustainable. This is yet another reason why Leonardo was aligned so closely with modern urban planning and centuries ahead of his time.

groups.desc:
Questions 27–33

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 27-33 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: People first referred to Leonardo da Vinci as a genius 500 years ago.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“People first referred to Leonardo da Vinci as a genius 500 years ago”，意思是“人们在500年前首次称达芬奇为天才”。然而，原文中虽然提到“genius”这个词与达芬奇的名字普遍相关联，并且提到达芬奇去世已经五百年，但并没有具体说明这种关联是在500年前开始的。因此，题干中的信息在文章中并没有明确提到，所以答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The word 'genius' is universally associated with the name of Leonardo da Vinci. A true Renaissance man, he embodied scientific spirit, artistic talent and humanist sensibilities. Five hundred years have passed since Leonardo died in his home at Château du Clos Lucé, outside Tours, France.
central_sentences.translation: "天才"这个词与列奥纳多·达·芬奇的名字普遍相关联。作为一个真正的文艺复兴时期的人，他体现了科学精神、艺术才能和人文主义情怀。自达芬奇在法国图尔市郊外的克洛吕塞城堡的家中去世以来，已经过去了500年。

target_sen.target_sen: The word 'genius' is universally associated with the name of Leonardo da Vinci.
Five hundred years have passed since Leonardo died in his home at Château du Clos Lucé, outside Tours, France.

questions.title: The current climate crisis is predicted to cause more deaths than the plague.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“当前的气候危机预计会导致比瘟疫更多的死亡”。在原文中，提到了气候危机会导致“广泛的流离失所、物种灭绝和死亡”，但没有具体提到死亡人数是否超过瘟疫（200 million deaths）。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到明确的对应，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed.
central_sentences.translation: 如今，世界正处于气候危机的临界点，如果不加以解决，预计将导致大规模的流离失所、物种灭绝和死亡。

central_sentences.sentence: The Renaissance marked the transition from the 15th century to modernity and took place after the spread of the plague in the 14th century, which caused a global crisis resulting in some 200 million deaths across Europe and Asia.
central_sentences.translation: 文艺复兴标志着从15世纪到现代的过渡，发生在14世纪黑死病蔓延之后，这场瘟疫导致了全球性危机，造成了欧洲和亚洲约2亿人死亡。

syn_ary.synonym: Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis

target_sen.target_sen: Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed.
Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed.

syn_ary.synonym: the spread of the plague in the 14th century

target_sen.target_sen: The Renaissance marked the transition from the 15th century to modernity and took place after the spread of the plague in the 14th century, which caused a global crisis resulting in some 200 million deaths across Europe and Asia.

The Renaissance marked the transition from the 15th century to modernity and took place after the spread of the plague in the 14th century, which caused a global crisis resulting in some 200 million deaths across Europe and Asia.

questions.title: Some of the challenges we face today can be compared to those of earlier times.
questions.analysis: 这道题的答案是 TRUE，因为文章中明确提到今天的气候危机和历史上的瘟疫都需要激进的解决方案来保护人类免受灾难。这表明现代的挑战可以与早期的挑战相比较。题干中的 "challenges we face today" 对应文章中的 "climate crisis"，"earlier times" 对应 "Then"，"compared" 对应 "as"。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找到了明确的对应，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed. Then, as now, radical solutions were called for to revolutionise the way people lived and safeguard humanity against catastrophe.
central_sentences.translation: 今天，世界正处于气候危机的边缘，如果不加以解决，预计将导致广泛的流离失所、物种灭绝和死亡。无论是过去还是现在，都需要采取激进的解决方案来彻底改变人们的生活方式，以保护人类免受灾难。

target_sen.target_sen: Today, the world is on the cusp of a climate crisis, which is predicted to cause widespread displacement, extinctions and death, if left unaddressed.

The Renaissance marked the transition from the 15th century to modernity and took place after the spread of the plague in the 14th century, which caused a global crisis resulting in some 200 million deaths across Europe and Asia.
Then, as now, radical solutions were called for to revolutionise the way people lived and safeguard humanity against catastrophe.

questions.title: Leonardo da Vinci's 'ideal city' was constructed in the 15th century.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“达芬奇的‘理想城市’在15世纪被建造”，而原文明确提到“理想城市”项目由于成本过高未能实现（would remain unfulfilled）。因此，题干中的信息是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Following a typical Renaissance trend, he began to work on an 'ideal city' project, which – due to its excessive costs – would remain unfulfilled.
central_sentences.translation: 按照典型的文艺复兴趋势，他开始了一个"理想城市"项目，但由于成本过高，这个项目未能实现。

target_sen.target_sen: Following a typical Renaissance trend, he began to work on an 'ideal city' project, which – due to its excessive costs – would remain unfulfilled.
Following a typical Renaissance trend, he began to work on an 'ideal city' project, which – due to its excessive costs – would remain unfulfilled.
Following a typical Renaissance trend, he began to work on an 'ideal city' project, which – due to its excessive costs – would remain unfulfilled.

questions.title: Poor town planning is a major contributor to climate change.
questions.analysis: 答案是TRUE，因为题干中的“poor town planning”在原文中被同义替换为“unsustainable urban models”，“major contributor to climate change”被同义替换为“key cause of global climate change”。原文明确指出，不可持续的城市模式是全球气候变化的主要原因之一，这与题干的意思完全一致。因此，题干中的陈述是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Yet given that unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today, it's only natural to wonder how Leonardo might have changed the shape of modern cities.
central_sentences.translation: 考虑到当今不可持续的城市模式是全球气候变化的一个主要原因，我们很自然地会想知道达芬奇可能会如何改变现代城市的形态。

target_sen.target_sen: Yet given that unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today, it's only natural to wonder how Leonardo might have changed the shape of modern cities.
Yet given that unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today, it's only natural to wonder how Leonardo might have changed the shape of modern cities.
Yet given that unsustainable urban models are a key cause of global climate change today, it's only natural to wonder how Leonardo might have changed the shape of modern cities.

questions.title: In Renaissance times, local people fought against the changes to Pienza and Ferrara.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在文章中没有提到。虽然文章提到了对皮恩扎和费拉拉的改变，但没有提到当地人是否反对这些改变。因此，正确答案是 "NOT GIVEN"。如果文章中没有提到相关信息，可以排除 "YES/TRUE" 和 "NO/FALSE" 选项，选择 "NOT GIVEN"。

central_sentences.sentence: The rigour and method behind the conscious conception of a city had been largely missing in Western thought until the moment when prominent Renaissance men pushed forward large-scale urban projects in Italy, such as the reconfiguration of the town of Pienza and the expansion of the city of Ferrara.
central_sentences.translation: 直到文艺复兴时期的著名人物在意大利推动大规模城市项目（如重新规划皮恩扎镇和扩建费拉拉市）时，西方思想中才开始真正具备有意识地构思城市的严谨性和方法。

target_sen.target_sen: Although the Renaissance is renowned as an era of incredible progress in art and architecture, it is rarely noted that the 15th century also marked the birth of urbanism as a true academic discipline.

The rigour and method behind the conscious conception of a city had been largely missing in Western thought until the moment when prominent Renaissance men pushed forward large-scale urban projects in Italy, such as the reconfiguration of the town of Pienza and the expansion of the city of Ferrara.

The rigour and method behind the conscious conception of a city had been largely missing in Western thought until the moment when prominent Renaissance men pushed forward large-scale urban projects in Italy, such as the reconfiguration of the town of Pienza and the expansion of the city of Ferrara.

The rigour and method behind the conscious conception of a city had been largely missing in Western thought until the moment when prominent Renaissance men pushed forward large-scale urban projects in Italy, such as the reconfiguration of the town of Pienza and the expansion of the city of Ferrara.

questions.title: Leonardo da Vinci kept a neat, organised record of his designs.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“neat, organised record”与原文中的“disordered way of working with notes and sketches”是相反的意思。题干说达芬奇的记录是整洁有条理的，而原文说他的工作方式是混乱的。因此，题干信息与文章信息是相反的，所以答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: It is not easy to identify a coordinated vision of Leonardo's ideal city because of his disordered way of working with notes and sketches.
central_sentences.translation: 由于达芬奇处理笔记和草图的方式杂乱无章，很难识别出他理想城市的协调愿景。

syn_ary.synonym: disordered way of working with notes and sketches

target_sen.target_sen: It is not easy to identify a coordinated vision of Leonardo's ideal city because of his disordered way of working with notes and sketches.

groups.title: Leonardo da Vinci's ideal city
groups.desc:
Questions 34–40

Complete the summary below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect:
A collection of Leonardo da Vinci's paperwork reveals his design of a new city beside the Ticino River. This was to provide better $74$for trade and a less polluted environment. Although Leonardo da Vinci's city shared many of the ideals of his time, some of his innovations were considered unconventional in their design. They included features that can be seen in some tower blocks today, such as $75$on the exterior of a building.

Leonardo da Vinci wasn't only an architect. His expertise in $76$was evident in his plans for artificial canals within his ideal city. He also believed that the height of houses should relate to the width of streets in case earthquakes occurred. The design of many cities in Italy today follows this $77$.

While some cities from $78$times have aspects that can also be found in Leonardo's designs, his ideas weren't put into practice until long after his death. $79$is one example of a city that was redesigned in the 19th century in the way that Leonardo had envisaged. His ideas are also relevant to today's world, where building $80$no longer seems to be the best approach.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“better”对应原文中的“easy”，“trade”对应“goods”，“less polluted environment”对应“clean urban spaces”。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案句是“he designed the city for the easy transport of goods and clean urban spaces”。其中，“easy transport”是提供更好贸易条件的具体措施，因此答案是“transport”。

central_sentences.sentence: He designed the city for the easy transport of goods and clean urban spaces
central_sentences.translation: 他设计这座城市是为了便于货物运输和保持城市空间的清洁。

target_sen.target_sen: He designed the city for the easy transport of goods and clean urban spaces, and he wanted a comfortable and spacious city, with well-ordered streets and architecture.
He designed the city for the easy transport of goods and clean urban spaces, and he wanted a comfortable and spacious city, with well-ordered streets and architecture.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "staircases"。题干中的 "tower blocks today" 和 "exterior of a building" 对应文章中的 "today's high-rise buildings" 和 "vertical outdoor"。通过这些同义表达，可以确定答案所在的句子是 "Leonardo wanted the city to be built on several levels, linked with vertical outdoor staircases."。因此，"staircases" 是正确答案，因为它符合题干中描述的特征。

central_sentences.sentence: Leonardo wanted the city to be built on several levels, linked with vertical outdoor staircases.
central_sentences.translation: 达芬奇希望城市建在几个层级上，用垂直的户外楼梯连接。

target_sen.target_sen: This design can be seen in some of today's high-rise buildings but was unconventional at the time.
Leonardo wanted the city to be built on several levels, linked with vertical outdoor staircases.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“His expertise in 【 】 was evident in his plans for artificial canals within his ideal city.”要求我们找到莱昂纳多在设计人工运河时体现的专长。通过定位“expertise”和“artificial canals”这两个线索词，我们在原文中找到了对应的表达“fusion of architecture and engineering”和“create artificial canals”。从句子“Leonardo's vision was its fusion of architecture and engineering”可以看出，莱昂纳多的专长在于“engineering”（工程学），这与题干中的要求完全吻合。因此，正确答案是“engineering”。

central_sentences.sentence: But the true originality of Leonardo's vision was its fusion of architecture and engineering. Leonardo designed extensive hydraulic plants to create artificial canals throughout the city.
central_sentences.translation: 但莱昂纳多愿景的真正独创性在于它融合了建筑学和工程学。莱昂纳多设计了大规模的水利设施，在整个城市创造人工运河。

target_sen.target_sen: But the true originality of Leonardo's vision was its fusion of architecture and engineering.
Leonardo designed extensive hydraulic plants to create artificial canals throughout the city.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "rule"。题干中的 "The design of many cities in Italy today follows this" 与原文中的 "a rule still followed in many contemporary cities across Italy" 是同义表达。题干问的是 "遵循这个"，而原文明确提到 "a rule still followed"，所以答案是 "rule"。

central_sentences.sentence: Leonardo also thought that the width of the streets ought to match the average height of the adjacent houses: a rule still followed in many contemporary cities across Italy, to allow access to sun and reduce the risk of damage from earthquakes.
central_sentences.translation: 列奥纳多还认为街道的宽度应该与相邻房屋的平均高度相匹配：这是一条在意大利许多现代城市中仍然遵循的规则，目的是为了保证阳光照射并降低地震造成损害的风险。

target_sen.target_sen: Leonardo also thought that the width of the streets ought to match the average height of the adjacent houses: a rule still followed in many contemporary cities across Italy, to allow access to sun and reduce the risk of damage from earthquakes.

locating_words.locating_word: design of many cities in Italy today

target_sen.target_sen: Leonardo also thought that the width of the streets ought to match the average height of the adjacent houses: a rule still followed in many contemporary cities across Italy, to allow access to sun and reduce the risk of damage from earthquakes.

questions.analysis: 答案是“Roman”，因为题干中的“some cities from 【 】times”对应文章中的“some of these features existed in Roman cities”，而“put into practice”对应“applied”，“long after his death”对应“it wasn't until the 19th century”。这些对应关系表明，尽管罗马城市中存在一些特征，但达芬奇的设计在他死后很久才被应用。因此，答案是“Roman”。

central_sentences.sentence: Although some of these features existed in Roman cities, before Leonardo's drawings there had never been a multi-level, compact modern city which was thoroughly technically conceived. Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然一些这样的特征在罗马城市中就已存在，但在莱昂纳多的图纸之前，从未有过一个经过全面技术构思的多层紧凑型现代城市。事实上，直到19世纪，他的一些想法才得以应用。

target_sen.target_sen: Although some of these features existed in Roman cities, before Leonardo's drawings there had never been a multi-level, compact modern city which was thoroughly technically conceived.
Although some of these features existed in Roman cities, before Leonardo's drawings there had never been a multi-level, compact modern city which was thoroughly technically conceived.
Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied.
Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "Paris"。题干中提到的 "redesigned in the 19th century" 对应原文中的 "renovation" 和 "19th century"，而 "in the way that Leonardo had envisaged" 对应原文中的 "some of his ideas were applied"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确定答案在文章中提到的 "Paris"。原文明确指出，巴黎在19世纪的改造中应用了达芬奇的一些理念，因此答案是 "Paris"。

central_sentences.sentence: Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied. For example, the subdivision of the city by function – with services and infrastructures located in the lower levels and wide and well-ventilated boulevards and walkways above for residents – is an idea that can be found in Georges-Eugène Haussmann's renovation of Paris under Emperor Napoleon III between 1853 and 1870.
central_sentences.translation: 事实上，直到19世纪，他的一些想法才得到应用。例如，根据功能对城市进行划分——在较低层设置服务设施和基础设施，在上层为居民设置宽敞通风的林荫大道和人行道——这个想法可以在乔治-欧仁·奥斯曼在拿破仑三世统治下对巴黎进行的改造中找到。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, the subdivision of the city by function – with services and infrastructures located in the lower levels and wide and well-ventilated boulevards and walkways above for residents – is an idea that can be found in Georges-Eugène Haussmann's renovation of Paris under Emperor Napoleon III between 1853 and 1870.
Indeed, it wasn't until the 19th century that some of his ideas were applied.
Although some of these features existed in Roman cities, before Leonardo's drawings there had never been a multi-level, compact modern city which was thoroughly technically conceived.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "building 【 】 no longer seems to be the best approach" 对应原文中的 "built upwards instead of outwards"。这里的 "instead of" 表示对比，说明向外扩展（outwards）不再是最佳方法。因此，正确答案是 "outwards"。

central_sentences.sentence: Many scholars think that the compact city, built upwards instead of outwards, integrated with nature (especially water systems), with efficient transport infrastructure, could help modern cities become more efficient and sustainable.
central_sentences.translation: 许多学者认为，紧凑型城市（向上而非向外建设，与自然融合（尤其是水系统），并具有高效的交通基础设施）可以帮助现代城市变得更加高效和可持续。

target_sen.target_sen: Many scholars think that the compact city, built upwards instead of outwards, integrated with nature (especially water systems), with efficient transport infrastructure, could help modern cities become more efficient and sustainable.

Many scholars think that the compact city, built upwards instead of outwards, integrated with nature (especially water systems), with efficient transport infrastructure, could help modern cities become more efficient and sustainable.
